text: The RLCS software shall send to and receive data from the field device I/O cards through I/O driver software.
The RLCS status for use by external systems shall be updated on the external server data store once per minute.
The RLCS shall provide a Graphical User Interface (GUI) that allows the operator to view system status, issue commands, configure the system, export log data, and generate reports.
The RLCS GUI shall indicate the current date and time, user name, and workstation location name.
The RLCS GUI shall display information about active overrides and devices with no active rules protection.
The RLCS GUI shall issue an audible alarm and change the device icon color when an alarm status is detected.
The RLCS shall update status information on the workstation display every 2 seconds.
The RLCS shall provide a configuration option accessible only by users with System Administrative privileges.
The RLCS shall analyze modified database table data for conflicting or redundant entries before storage.
The RLCS shall display information logs and provide the capability to export logs in ASCII text format.
The RLCS shall request confirmation of a command via a separate window upon receipt of a manual or scheduled command.
The RLCS shall report any change in device state on the screen no later than 2 seconds from the time of occurrence.
The RLCS shall abort a device control command if any closure control device status is unknown.
The RLCS shall generate critical alarms if a closure device changes from a known state to an unknown state.
The RLCS shall generate a warning alarm upon security sensor activation at either the FCU or DCU.
The RLCS shall validate each control command against secured safety rules stored in non-volatile memory.
The RLCS shall execute stored operational control command sequences based on the system mode and schedule.
The RLCS shall halt an opening or closing sequence if a device fails to report completion within the allotted response time window.
The RLCS shall employ the MD5 algorithm to secure application data and software in the controllers and application server.
The RLCS must function continuously without a reset or reboot due to software error for at least 30 consecutive days.
The RLCS must be available 24 hours per day, 7 days per week, 365 days per year.
The RLCS recovery time after a failure must be no greater than 10 minutes.
The RLCS shall support remote system administration and maintenance.
The RLCS shall utilize an open architecture that is modular and scaleable.
The RLCS shall provide a secure remote dial-in interface through a firewall via a dial-up modem.
The system shall support an administrator level.
